title: calamintha officinalis subsp. menthifolia
intro: Calamintha officinalis subsp. menthifolia, commonly known as mint calamintha or mountain balm, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, colds, and flu, due to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing respiratory issues. Calamintha officinalis subsp. menthifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
